title: Disapproving of the rule submitted by the Department of Homeland Security relating to "Modernizing H-1B Requirements, Providing Flexibility in the F-1 Program, and Program Improvements Affecting Other Nonimmigrant Workers".
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
plain_summary: This bill rejects a new rule from the Department of Homeland Security about work and student visas. The rule would have changed requirements for H-1B work visas, which are for workers with special skills, and F-1 student visas. The changes included making it harder to qualify for H-1B visas and letting some students work longer while waiting for their H-1B applications. Since Congress is rejecting this rule, none of these changes will happen.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: The Senate and House of Representatives have decided together that Congress does not approve of a rule created by the Department of Homeland Security. This rule was about updating requirements for H-1B visas (work permits for skilled foreign workers), making changes to F-1 programs (student visas), and improving programs for other temporary foreign workers. The rule was published in the Federal Register on a specific date with the number eighty-nine Fed. Reg. one hundred three thousand fifty-four. Because Congress disapproves of this rule, it will have no power and will not take effect.
